contentBlocks.title: When do you need to convert kg to lbs?
contentBlocks.html:
Kilogram (kg) is the base unit of mass in the metric system. Pound (lb) is the standard mass unit in the imperial system, used primarily in the US and UK. The conversion factor is: 1 kg = 2.20462 lbs.

Converting kg to lbs is essential for gym workouts (US gyms use pound plates), shipping and freight (international vs US weight limits), cooking with American recipes, and understanding body weight in US medical contexts.

Common examples: 70 kg = 154.3 lbs, 80 kg = 176.4 lbs, 100 kg = 220.5 lbs. A standard Olympic barbell weighs 20 kg (44.1 lbs).

All calculations run locally in your browser — nothing is sent to any server.

contentBlocks.title: How does kg to lbs conversion work?
contentBlocks.html:
The conversion is based on the international avoirdupois pound: 1 lb = 0.45359237 kg exactly. Therefore, 1 kg = 2.20462 lbs.

To convert kg to lbs, multiply by 2.20462. To convert lbs to kg, multiply by 0.45359237 (or divide by 2.20462).

Quick mental math: multiply kg by 2.2. Example: 75 kg × 2.2 = 165 lbs (exact: 165.35 lbs).

contentBlocks.title: Practical tips
contentBlocks.html:
• Body weight: 60 kg = 132 lbs, 70 kg = 154 lbs, 80 kg = 176 lbs, 90 kg = 198 lbs.
  • Gym plates: 20 kg = 44.1 lbs, 25 kg = 55.1 lbs. US plates are 45 lbs = 20.4 kg.
  • Luggage: 23 kg (economy) = 50.7 lbs. 32 kg (business) = 70.5 lbs.
  • 1 stone (UK) = 14 lbs = 6.35 kg. Height/weight in UK often uses stones.

items.question: How many pounds is 1 kg?
items.answer: 1 kg = 2.20462 pounds. This is based on the international avoirdupois pound definition.

items.question: How many kg is 150 lbs?
items.answer: 150 lbs = 68.04 kg. This is a common body weight conversion.

items.question: What is the quick way to convert kg to lbs?
items.answer: Multiply kg by 2.2. Example: 80 kg × 2.2 = 176 lbs (exact: 176.37 lbs). The error is less than 0.2%.

items.question: How much is a stone in kg?
items.answer: 1 stone = 14 pounds = 6.35 kg. Stones are used informally in the UK for body weight.

items.question: What is the luggage limit in kg?
items.answer: Most airlines allow 23 kg (50.7 lbs) for economy and 32 kg (70.5 lbs) for business/first class.

items.question: Are calculations done locally?
items.answer: Yes. All calculations run in your browser. No data is sent to any server.
